summary: Sync Figma design files, projects, team components, published styles, comments, and version history into Shielva via the Figma REST API using a Personal Access Token.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Figma connector provides read-only access to your Figma team's design assets. It traverses the team project hierarchy (team → projects → files → comments) and also fetches the team's published component and style libraries. Each resource is normalized into a ConnectorDocument with a stable SHA-256 derived ID so re-syncing the same file always produces the same document ID.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: Authentication uses a Figma Personal Access Token (PAT) transmitted via the X-Figma-Token header on every request. The config field is named 'api_key'. 'personal_access_token' is accepted as a legacy alias for backward compatibility. Generate a PAT in Figma → Account Settings → Security → Personal access tokens.

fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Figma PAT — transmitted as X-Figma-Token header

fields.label: Team ID
fields.description: Numeric team ID from the Figma URL: figma.com/files/team/{team_id}/. Required to access team projects, components, and styles.

methods.name: get_me
methods.description: Fetch authenticated user info — used for health check

methods.name: list_projects(team_id)
methods.description: List all projects for a team

methods.name: list_files(project_id)
methods.description: List all files in a project

methods.name: get_file(file_key)
methods.description: Fetch the full document tree for a file

methods.name: get_file_nodes(file_key, node_ids)
methods.description: Fetch specific nodes within a file by node ID list

methods.name: get_file_comments(file_key)
methods.description: List all comments on a file

methods.name: get_file_versions(file_key)
methods.description: List version history of a file

methods.name: get_team_components(team_id)
methods.description: List published components for a team (cursor-paginated)

methods.name: get_team_styles(team_id)
methods.description: List published styles for a team (cursor-paginated)

exceptions.name: FigmaError
exceptions.role: Base exception for all Figma connector errors

exceptions.name: FigmaAuthError
exceptions.role: Raised on 401/403 or 200+{status:403} — invalid or expired PAT. Not retried.

exceptions.name: FigmaNetworkError
exceptions.role: Raised on connection/timeout failures and 5xx responses

exceptions.name: FigmaNotFoundError
exceptions.role: Raised on 404 — resource deleted or not accessible. Not retried.

items.solution: Regenerate the PAT in Figma Settings → Security. Ensure the PAT belongs to an account with access to the target team.
Verify the Team ID is the numeric ID from the Figma URL (not the team name). The PAT account must be a team member.

items.issue: Components or styles list is empty
items.solution: Components and styles must be explicitly published to the team library (right-click → Publish in Figma) before they appear in the API.
Figma sometimes returns {status: 403, err: '...'} inside a 200 response. The connector correctly detects this and raises FigmaAuthError.
Reduce sync frequency or use a dedicated Figma service account. The connector retries transient errors with exponential backoff but does not auto-retry 429.

auth.token_source: Figma → Account Settings → Security → Personal access tokens
